opinion.h: KIRKLAND v. TEXAS. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF CRIMINAL APPEALS OF TEXAS. No. 465. Decided December 2, 1963.
opinion.p: The appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h: ABERNATHY v. EASTERN AIR LINES, INC., 375 U.S. 161 (1963)
opinion.p: 375 U.S. 161 ABERNATHY ET AL. v. EASTERN AIR LINES, INC., ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF NORTH CAROLINA. No. 450. Decided December 2, 1963.*
[Footnote *] Together with No. 451, Charlotte Council, Air Line Pilots Association, et al. v. Eastern Air Lines, Inc., et al., also on appeal from the same Court.
Whiteford S. Blakeney for appellants in No. 450. Wade W. Mitchem for appellants in No. 451.
William D. Holoman for appellee Employment Security Commission of North Carolina.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeals are dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeals were taken as petitions for writs of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
